title: JARVIS Step 22 — Operator Guide / Runbook

baseline.step_20_purpose: Safe UI-only polish pass (templates/jarvis.html only): section dividers, posture legend, colour-coded tags, prominent Commander's Snapshot, regrouped Cache Freshness, roomier spacing. API shape unchanged.
baseline.step_21_purpose: Post-polish live smoke memo: confirmed the polished UI is live, all key panels render, API shape unchanged at 24 top-level keys, no execution/refresh controls. Conclusion JARVIS_POST_POLISH_SMOKE_PASS.

purpose: An operator-facing guide/runbook for the JARVIS Command Center: what the page shows, how to read each panel and section, the read-only safety posture, and what operators should and should not do. Documentation only — it adds no capability, control, or API change.
relation_to_steps_20_21: Step 20 polished the UI; Step 21 proved the polished UI is live and safe. Step 22 documents how to operate and interpret that polished, validated surface. No code, UI, or API is touched.

what_is_jarvis_page.description: A cinematic, read-only dashboard (single HTML page) that renders the aggregated SPARTA_BRAIN state. It performs one GET fetch to /api/jarvis/status and re-polls on a 15s timer. It has no buttons, forms, onclick handlers, POST actions, or refresh endpoint. The browser surface never mutates anything.

what_is_status_api.description: A GET-only JSON aggregate with 24 top-level keys: 22 read-only sections plus the two meta keys online and read_only. Every section is wrapped fail-closed by _jarvis_safe, so one failing section returns an error dict for that section but still yields HTTP 200. The endpoint reads state; it never executes scripts, runs probes, refreshes caches, or controls a broker.

field_meaning.read_only: true means the JARVIS surface is in its guaranteed observe-only posture: no execution, no refresh control, no broker/paper/live path, no file mutation from the web. read_only must always be true.
field_meaning.online: true means the JARVIS aggregator responded and the page received its status payload. It is a liveness signal for the dashboard itself, not a claim that every downstream subsystem is green.
field_meaning.commander_snapshot_overall_state_yellow: yellow is the conservative-by-design verdict, most commonly driven by the large untracked-file backlog and a dirty working tree. It means 'needs attention / not fully green' — it is NOT an error and NOT an approval to act. Green requires a clean posture; the required safety checks (safety locked, health pass, route smoke pass, caches fresh) can all be green while overall stays yellow purely due to the dirty/untracked working tree.

trading_detail.read_only: true — the trading surface is observe-only.
trading_detail.paper_ready: false — no paper-trading path is wired or armed.
trading_detail.live_ready: false — no live-trading path is wired or armed.
trading_detail.broker_control: false — JARVIS holds no broker control and exposes no order path. Research is candidate/visibility only.

ui_sections.purpose: Single-glance verdict: the Commander's Snapshot with the overall green/yellow/red state and contributing signals.
Server, AI brains, git repository state, project artifacts, and brain-memory tree.
Read-only engine status (trading bridge, content, money, moving) plus the daily mission and recommended next actions.
Safety gates, operator posture flags, and the cached health / route smoke / cache freshness / file hygiene reports.
Read-only trading research detail — no broker, no paper, no live.
Tracked, display-only mission board, prompt library, and system wiring map.

key_panels.title: Commander's Snapshot
key_panels.interpretation: Top-level verdict (green/yellow/red) plus safety, health, route smoke, trading posture, cache, mission/prompt counts, staged/untracked counts, and warnings. Yellow = needs attention, not approval.

key_panels.title: System Core
key_panels.interpretation: Server running state, brain state, server time, and library counts.

key_panels.title: AI Brains
key_panels.interpretation: Per-brain state (Claude/Codex/Gemini/Ollama). Warn/idle states are informational.

key_panels.title: Trading Bridge
key_panels.interpretation: READ-ONLY / LOCKED posture, sealed lifecycle count, and per-lifecycle verdicts. No order path.

key_panels.title: Health Report
key_panels.interpretation: CACHED offline compile/test health. Overall pass/fail plus py_compile and pytest results. 'No cached report' means the manual tool has not been run.

key_panels.title: Route Smoke
key_panels.interpretation: CACHED GET-only route check. Overall pass/fail and per-route status codes. /money-spartan may show a non-required 404.

key_panels.title: File Hygiene
key_panels.interpretation: CACHED git/untracked summary: untracked count, tracked-modified, staged count, top untracked dirs, and that cached reports stay gitignored. A non-zero staged count is worth a glance.

key_panels.title: Mission Board
key_panels.interpretation: DISPLAY-ONLY tracked operator missions with status/priority. 'Safe next prompt' text is display only — nothing executes it.

key_panels.title: Prompt Library
key_panels.interpretation: DISPLAY-ONLY tracked prompts with category/risk/allowed flags. Prompt text is shown, never run, and there is no copy-and-run affordance.

key_panels.title: System Map
key_panels.interpretation: DISPLAY-ONLY wiring map: panels (id/api_key/kind), manual-only scripts, tracked data files, ignored runtime files, and posture flags (read-only true, browser execution disabled, broker control disabled, file mutation from web disabled).

key_panels.title: Trading Research Detail
key_panels.interpretation: READ-ONLY research detail: candidate status, read_only/paper_ready/live_ready/broker_control flags, S26 research state, and latest report cards. No broker/paper/live.

key_panels.title: Cache Freshness
key_panels.interpretation: DISPLAY-ONLY cache metadata: overall fresh/stale/missing, per-cache age vs threshold, gitignored status, generated_at/modified_at. Reads metadata only; never runs the generator scripts and never refreshes a cache. Now grouped with Health Report and Route Smoke under Safety & Reliability.

safety_rules: JARVIS observes only — it is a read-only mirror of state.
JARVIS does not execute commands, scripts, prompts, or missions.
JARVIS does not refresh anything through UI controls; there is no refresh button or endpoint. Data updates only via the page's own GET poll.
JARVIS does not control broker, paper, or live trading, and exposes no order path.
Operators must treat yellow as 'needs attention / not fully green', NOT as approval to act.
Cached-report scripts (health / route smoke / file hygiene) are manual-only and are never run from the web.
operator_daily_checklist: Open http://127.0.0.1:8765/jarvis and confirm the page loads (status orb ONLINE, not SIGNAL LOST).
Confirm Commander's Snapshot renders; note overall state (green/yellow/red) and read any warnings.
Confirm Safety Gates show Trading execution LOCKED, YouTube upload APPROVAL_REQUIRED, Live automation BLOCKED.
Confirm Operator Safety flags are all locked/true (read-only, no execution control, no broker control, no secret display, no force git).
Confirm Trading Research Detail shows read_only TRUE and paper_ready / live_ready / broker_control all FALSE.
Glance at Cache Freshness: overall fresh; if stale/missing, that only means a manual cached report is old or not generated — not a live failure.
Glance at File Hygiene staged count; a non-zero staged count is worth investigating before any commit work.
Optionally confirm GET /api/jarvis/status returns 200 with online=true, read_only=true, and 24 top-level keys.

failure_interpretation.symptom: A panel is missing or stuck on its loading text
failure_interpretation.meaning: The page did not receive that section, or that section returned an error dict (fail-closed). The endpoint still returns 200. Reload the page; if it persists, inspect /api/jarvis/status for that section's error field.
failure_interpretation.action: Investigate the named section offline. Do not add any control to 'fix' it from the UI.

failure_interpretation.meaning: The server is down or the route is broken.
failure_interpretation.action: Restart the server process offline (manual), then re-check. Do not expose a restart/refresh control in the UI.

failure_interpretation.meaning: The aggregator did not report itself online; the dashboard liveness signal failed.
failure_interpretation.action: Treat as degraded; investigate the server/aggregator offline.

failure_interpretation.meaning: CRITICAL — the read-only guarantee is broken. This must never happen for the JARVIS surface.
failure_interpretation.action: Stop. Treat as a serious safety regression, investigate immediately, and do not trust the surface until read_only is true again.

failure_interpretation.meaning: CRITICAL — a broker control path is reported present. JARVIS must never hold broker control.
failure_interpretation.action: Stop. Treat as a serious safety regression; halt and investigate before any further use.

failure_interpretation.meaning: CRITICAL — a paper or live trading path is reported armed/ready. Both must stay false on the JARVIS surface.
failure_interpretation.action: Stop. Treat as a serious safety regression; halt and investigate before any further use.

recommended_next_safe_improvements: JARVIS-CACHE-FRESHNESS-AGE-BADGES — optional display-only age badges per cache row (no new controls).
JARVIS-DOCS-AUTO-CHECK-MEMO — a read-only memo that re-verifies the docs index against the live API and tracked docs.
JARVIS-LIVE-REGRESSION-SMOKE (recurring) — re-run the live regression / post-polish smoke memo after future JARVIS commits.
JARVIS-OPERATOR-GUIDE-LINK — optionally surface a display-only link/footnote pointing operators to this runbook (no behavior).
conclusion: Documentation-only operator guide created. No code, UI, tool, test, API, or trading surface was changed.
